Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Jan-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Robert J. Hatem of KLINEDINST PC (213-406-1100) appearing for defendant / moving party Dixieline Lumber Company
Barry Matson of GOLD LAW FIRM (415-986-1338, X-122), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Paul Edward Fort and Carolyn Fort

RULING:
DEFENDANT DIXIELINE LUMBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER STRIKING PORTIONS OF MOTION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Defendant Dixieline Lumber Company's motion to strike is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiffs' complaint pleads facts sufficient to state a claim for punitive damages and is denied upon such ground. Plaintiffs are granted 10 days leave to amend to properly specify upon which cause(s) of action they are stating a claim for punitive damages. The first amended complaint is deemed served on all defendants previously appearing herein and having already answered the complaint, as of the date the first amended complaint is filed; and that defendants' previous answers are deemed answers to the first amended complaint, and that all general denials and affirmative defenses previously pleaded shall apply. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
